perturbation.original_text: Any Information which, after the disclosure, enters the public domain without any action or fault of Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: Any Information which, after the disclosure, enters the public domain due to Recipient's negligence.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'without any action or fault of Recipient' to 'due to Recipient's negligence' creates an in-text contradiction. Originally, any information entering the public domain was excluded from non-disclosure obligations regardless of the reason. Now, only information entering the public domain due to negligence is excluded, contradicting the broader exclusion principle. This in-text contradiction relates to any section discussing liabilities related to a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of any breach of this Agreement by either party, or any threatened or attempted breach by either party, the other party to this Agreement shall be entitled to an injunction against the breaching party. Nothing contained herein precludes the other party from pursuing any other remedies available hereunder or at law or equity for such breach, including the recovery of damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that any information entering the public domain, regardless of the recipient's fault, was excluded from non-disclosure obligations. The changed text narrows this exclusion to only information entering the public domain due to the recipient's negligence. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that any breach of the agreement entitles the other party to remedies, including damages. The change makes it unclear whether a breach occurs if information enters the public domain due to a reason other than the Recipient's negligence, creating an inconsistency regarding liabilities and enforceability related to the breach. This is a clear and impactful contradiction as it directly affects the scope of liability and enforcement, making the contract inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: If Disclosing Party gives its written consent to Recipient to disclose Information or Items to a third party, Recipient shall require the third party to execute a non-disclosure agreement in form, and substance satisfactory to Disclosing Party. Recipient shall promptly furnish Disclosing Party with a copy of all such non-disclosure agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: If Disclosing Party gives its written consent to Recipient to disclose Information or Items to a third party, Recipient is encouraged to have the third party execute a non-disclosure agreement. Recipient should furnish Disclosing Party with a copy of such non-disclosure agreements, if obtained.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall require' to 'is encouraged to have' and 'shall promptly furnish' to 'should furnish, if obtained' weakens the obligation for the Recipient to ensure a third party signs an NDA and to provide a copy. This creates a conflict because the original wording implies a mandatory requirement, while the altered text makes it optional. This in-text contradiction is related to legal liabilities related to non-disclosure and disclosure requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of any breach of this Agreement by either party, or any threatened or attempted breach by either party, the other party to this Agreement shall be entitled to an injunction against the breaching party. Nothing contained herein precludes the other party from pursuing any other remedies available hereunder or at law or equity for such breach, including the recovery of damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the recipient to ensure a third party signs an NDA and provide a copy to the disclosing party. The changed text weakens this obligation, making it optional. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that 'In the event of any breach of this Agreement,' the non-breaching party is entitled to an injunction and other remedies. By weakening the obligation to secure an NDA from third parties, the 'Changed Text' increases the risk of a breach (disclosure of confidential information by a third party) which can lead to legal liabilities related to non-disclosure and disclosure requirements, creating a conflict because the original wording implies a mandatory requirement, while the altered text makes it optional, contradicting the remedies available stated in the 'Contradicted Text'. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: In the event you are required by any legal or civil proceeding to disclose the Information or Items to a third party, you agree to provide prompt notice to the Disclosing Party, so that we might attempt to secure an appropriate protective order, or waive your compliance with this Agreement. You agree to cooperate with the Disclosing Party in its attempts to secure such a protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event you are required by any legal or civil proceeding to disclose the Information or Items to a third party, you may, at your discretion, provide notice to the Disclosing Party. You are encouraged to cooperate with the Disclosing Party in its attempts to secure a protective order.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agree to provide prompt notice' to 'may, at your discretion, provide notice' and 'agree to cooperate' to 'are encouraged to cooperate' weakens the mandatory nature of the notification and cooperation in case of legal disclosure. This creates an in-text contradiction by giving the Recipient the option to notify rather than requiring it, which is contradictory to the overall intention of protecting the Disclosing Party's information. This contradicts the general obligations of the Recipient stated in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three (3) years from the date of entering in to this Agreement, not to disclosure any Information or Item, in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, whether in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party, to any individual, firm or entity, excepting solely an individual <PAGE> 2 who (a) is an officer, director or employee of Disclosing Party or of the Recipient and (b) has a clear need to know for purposes directly related to the Matter.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates notification and cooperation in case of legal disclosure. The changed text weakens this to a discretionary notification and encouragement to cooperate. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which states 'not to disclosure any Information or Item, in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly...without the prior written consent'. By making notification discretionary, the Recipient could potentially disclose information without prior written consent and without even notifying the Disclosing Party, directly contradicting the non-disclosure obligation. This is a strong contradiction as it fundamentally alters the protection afforded to the Disclosing Party's information and impacts the enforceability of the non-disclosure agreement.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that you will only discuss the Matter with Mr. Al Weber, Chief Executive Officer, or Mr. Thomas V. Gilboy, Chief Financial Officer of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that you will primarily discuss the Matter with Mr. Al Weber, Chief Executive Officer, or Mr. Thomas V. Gilboy, Chief Financial Officer of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will only discuss' to 'will primarily discuss' creates ambiguity and an in-text contradiction. The original clause strictly limits discussion to two individuals. The altered clause suggests they are the preferred contacts, but does not forbid discussions with others. This contradiction undermines the control over information dissemination. This creates a contradiction with the non-disclosure agreement mentioned in section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three (3) years from the date of entering in to this Agreement, not to disclosure any Information or Item, in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, whether in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party, to any individual, firm or entity, excepting solely an individual <PAGE> 2 who (a) is an officer, director or employee of Disclosing Party or of the Recipient and (b) has a clear need to know for purposes directly related to the Matter.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly limits discussion to two individuals (Mr. Weber and Mr. Gilboy). The changed text, by using 'primarily discuss', implies that discussion with other individuals is possible. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that no information can be disclosed to any individual without prior written consent, except for those who are officers, directors, or employees of Disclosing Party or of the Recipient and has a clear need to know for purposes directly related to the Matter. The changed text broadens the scope of permissible discussions beyond the two named individuals, thus contradicting the strict non-disclosure requirement of the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of two (2) years Recipient (or any agent or affiliates of the Recipient) will not have discussions with any "Covered Employees" of the Disclosing Party regarding employment opportunities with the Recipient or any of its affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of two (2) years Recipient (or any agent or affiliates of the Recipient) will attempt to avoid discussions with any "Covered Employees" of the Disclosing Party regarding employment opportunities with the Recipient or any of its affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will not have discussions' to 'will attempt to avoid discussions' weakens the restriction on employee poaching. Originally, the contract explicitly prohibited such discussions. Now, the obligation is only to 'attempt to avoid' them, creating an in-text contradiction. This weakened obligation relates to any sections relying on strong adherence to the non-solicitation clause and creates ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of any breach of this Agreement by either party, or any threatened or attempted breach by either party, the other party to this Agreement shall be entitled to an injunction against the breaching party. Nothing contained herein precludes the other party from pursuing any other remedies available hereunder or at law or equity for such breach, including the recovery of damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibited discussions about employment, providing a clear basis for injunctive relief as stated in the contradicted text. The changed text weakens this prohibition to 'attempt to avoid discussions,' creating ambiguity. This directly contradicts the contradicted text's statement that 'any breach' entitles the other party to an injunction, because the 'attempt to avoid' wording makes it harder to prove a breach and thus to obtain an injunction, creating an in-text contradiction as the obligation has been lessened.
